description: You receive an email asking for your login credentials urgently.
explanation: Emails requesting sensitive info are classic phishing attempts.

description: A USB labeled 'Salary Info' is left in the parking lot.
explanation: Baiting uses curiosity to trick users into using malicious devices.

description: Website traffic spikes from multiple IPs, slowing services.
explanation: High traffic from multiple sources causing downtime is a DDoS attack.

description: An unknown caller claims to be IT support requesting your password.
explanation: Phone calls impersonating staff to steal credentials are vishing attacks.

description: Input fields allow database commands to be executed.
explanation: Poor input validation letting attackers run SQL commands is SQL injection.

description: Strangers enter office claiming to be delivery personnel and take photos.
explanation: Unauthorized people in restricted areas constitute a physical breach.

description: Ads on trusted sites redirect users to harmful software downloads.
explanation: Malicious ads redirecting users to harmful content is malvertising.

description: An email pretends to be from the CEO asking for a wire transfer.
explanation: BEC targets businesses using spoofed executive emails for fraud.

description: Software monitors keystrokes and screenshots without consent.
explanation: Monitoring tools installed secretly are spyware or keyloggers.

description: Company files are encrypted and a ransom is demanded in crypto.
explanation: Encrypting files and demanding payment is ransomware.

description: SMS asks you to click a link to verify account info.
explanation: Phishing via SMS is called smishing.

description: Someone observes your password entry over your shoulder.
explanation: Watching someone enter credentials is shoulder surfing.

description: Malware hides in system files to avoid detection.
explanation: Rootkits hide in system files to maintain control stealthily.

description: A program activates when a certain date occurs.
explanation: Code triggered by events like dates is a logic bomb.

description: An attacker intercepts communication between you and a website.
explanation: Man-in-the-middle attacks intercept and manipulate communications.

description: Random strangers dig through trash for confidential documents.
explanation: Attackers recover sensitive info from discarded materials.

description: Unauthorized person follows an employee into a restricted area.
explanation: Entering secure areas by following someone is tailgating.

description: A malicious email attachment installs hidden malware.
explanation: Malware disguised as legitimate software is a Trojan.

description: Hackers send repeated login attempts to guess passwords.
explanation: Guessing passwords through repeated attempts is brute force attack.

description: Website displays injected malicious scripts to other users.
explanation: XSS allows attackers to inject scripts into web pages viewed by others.

description: A hacker scans the network for open ports and services.
explanation: Scanning a network for vulnerabilities is network scanning.

description: Software encrypts your files silently and deletes itself afterward.
explanation: Wiper malware destroys data instead of demanding ransom.

description: Hackers trick users into revealing personal info by posing as authority.
explanation: Attackers create fake scenarios to extract information.

description: Email targets a specific individual with tailored content.
explanation: Spear phishing attacks are personalized to the victim.

description: High-level executives are targeted with fake urgent emails.
explanation: Whaling targets high-profile individuals with customized attacks.

description: Unauthorized script runs to redirect users or steal data.
explanation: CSRF forces users to execute unwanted actions on web apps.

description: Fake Wi-Fi hotspot used to intercept data.
explanation: Fake access points trick users into sending data through attackers.

description: A program installs malware on other computers when run.
explanation: Worms self-replicate to infect other systems.

description: An attacker sends fake messages to manipulate users into transferring money.
explanation: BEC involves fraudulent emails requesting financial transactions.

description: Malicious website secretly collects data from users’ browsers.
explanation: Visiting a site can trigger automatic malware downloads.

description: Software takes control of your computer and locks it until payment.
explanation: Encrypting files and demanding payment is ransomware behavior.

description: Attackers inject malicious code into a website to alter its behavior.
explanation: XSS allows code execution in the victim's browser via a trusted site.

description: Fake software updates install malware.
explanation: Trojan disguises malware as legitimate updates.

description: Repeated traffic overwhelms a server causing downtime.
explanation: Distributed traffic overloads services causing disruption.

description: Logs reveal attempts to guess multiple user passwords rapidly.
explanation: Repeated login attempts are indicative of brute force attacks.

description: User is redirected from a normal site to a malicious one via an ad.
explanation: Ads on trusted sites redirecting users to malware is malvertising.

description: A hacker uses a fake identity to access confidential data.
explanation: Creating a false scenario to gain trust and access data is pretexting.

description: Multiple failed login attempts indicate automated hacking attempts.
explanation: Automation to guess passwords is brute force.

description: Cybercriminal gains access to data and threatens release unless paid.
explanation: Encrypting files and extorting money is ransomware.

description: An attacker injects harmful SQL commands into a web form.
explanation: SQL injection manipulates database commands through input.

description: Email asks to reset password on a fake login page.
explanation: Fake login pages steal credentials via phishing emails.

description: User connects to a rogue Wi-Fi hotspot that intercepts data.
explanation: Fake Wi-Fi access points capture sensitive traffic.

description: An attacker tricks employees to plug in infected USBs.
explanation: Curiosity is used to get victims to execute malware from USBs.

description: Malware spreads by copying itself to other machines automatically.
explanation: Worms self-replicate to infect other systems.

description: Sensitive info is stolen by digging through office trash.
explanation: Retrieving confidential info from trash is dumpster diving.

description: User credentials are intercepted over an unsecure network.
explanation: Intercepting communications is a man-in-the-middle attack.

description: An attacker manipulates users to perform actions unknowingly on a site.
explanation: CSRF exploits trust of a website in the user’s browser.
